mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between RSAFS month-over-month growth and subsequent 20-day SPX return falls below 0.1 for any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.5%, it signals labor market weakness and increasing recession risk, which depresses equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances where UNRATE > 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 4% year-over-year and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is below the inflation rate, real rates are deeply negative, fueling speculation that will reverse as the central bank tightens, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 4 and FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL YoY
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint trigger condition with subsequent 60-day SPX return remains below -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In the late-cycle expansion phase, decelerating industrial production growth (INDPRO) signals diminishing economic momentum and impending contraction, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO QoQ growth < 2% annualized after previous two quarters averaged > 4%
failure_condition: In periods meeting the late-cycle deceleration condition, average 20-day SPX return is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1.2 million units indicates a housing market contraction that propagates to broader economic weakness, reducing equity prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered events when HOUST falls below 1200.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly increases in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) reflect a strengthening labor market, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year correlation between monthly PAYEMS change and subsequent 60-day SPX return is below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: When both the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is below 4% and the fed funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is below 3%, the economy exhibits tight labor markets yet still accommodative monetary policy, a combination that historically precedes above-average short-term equity gains over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day SPX returns are negative in more than 50% of periods meeting both conditions.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, when the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is above 4% and rising month-over-month, the tightening reduces liquidity and raises discount rates, causing a negative reaction in S&P 500 prices over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4 AND FEDFUNDS > previous month value
failure_condition: In periods meeting tightening conditions, forward 5-day SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of cases.
